summary: The Egyptian Hallel and companion praise psalms (111–118) span acrostic wisdom, the Exodus, a polemic against dead idols, thanksgiving for deliverance from death, and the rejected-cornerstone prophecy that Jesus applied directly to himself.
psalm_rows_html:
PsalmTypeAuthorSituationKey ThemeNotable VerseNT Connection
111Praise / AcrosticAnonymousCorporate worshipGod's works are great and worthy of study; his righteousness endures foreverv.10 — “The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom”Echoes throughout Proverbs and James 1:5
112Wisdom / AcrosticAnonymousInstruction on the blessed lifeThe man who fears the LORD mirrors God's own righteousness and generosityv.4 — “Gracious, merciful, and righteous”2 Cor 9:9 quotes v.9 regarding generous giving
113Praise (Egyptian Hallel)AnonymousOpening of Passover HallelGod is enthroned above all yet stoops to lift the lowly and barrenv.5–6 — “Who is like the LORD our God...”Luke 1:52–53 (Magnificat) echoes vv.7–9
114Historical praise (Egyptian Hallel)AnonymousRecalling the ExodusAll creation trembled at God's presence when he led Israel out of Egyptv.7 — “Tremble, O earth, at the presence of the LORD”Paul's “rock that followed them” (1 Cor 10:4) echoes v.8
115Liturgical praise (Egyptian Hallel)AnonymousContrast between Israel's living God and pagan idolsIdols are dead and make their worshipers like them; Israel trusts the living Godv.8 — “Those who make them become like them”Rom 1:22–23 echoes the idol polemic
116Individual thanksgiving (Egyptian Hallel)AnonymousDeliverance from near-deathGod hears the cry of the afflicted and delivers; death of the saints is precious to himv.15 — “Precious in the sight of the LORD is the death of his saints”“Cup of salvation” (v.13) echoes the Lord's Supper language
117Praise (Egyptian Hallel)AnonymousUniversal call to worshipAll nations called to praise the LORD for his steadfast love and faithfulnessv.2 — “Great is his steadfast love toward us”Quoted by Paul in Rom 15:11 as proof of Gentile inclusion
118Thanksgiving / Messianic (Egyptian Hallel)AnonymousClosing Passover Hallel; royal/national deliveranceThe rejected stone becomes the cornerstone; this is the LORD's doingv.22–23 — “The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone”Quoted by Jesus (Matt 21:42) and Peter (Acts 4:11; 1 Pet 2:7); “Hosanna” / “Blessed is he who comes” (v.26) sung on Palm Sunday
